interfaces.name: Provisioner._get_base_roles
interfaces.behavior_change: Append 'opencode' to the role list when 'opencode' in self.config.frameworks. Mirrors the playwright branch but does NOT auto-add 'node' (opencode is a static binary).

interfaces.name: Provisioner.run (NFR5 distro guard)
interfaces.behavior_change: Before generating the playbook, raise ConfigValidationError when self.config.vm_distro == 'alpine' and 'opencode' in self.config.frameworks. Message must reference '--distro ubuntu' and explain that opencode requires Ubuntu (pre-Alpine-removal targeting). Guard runs BEFORE _get_base_roles so the error fires before any role lookup.

interfaces.name: _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS (module-level frozenset)
interfaces.behavior_change: Add 'opencode' so _apply_distro_suffix maps 'opencode' -> 'opencode-ubuntu' for ubuntu (and 'opencode-alpine' for alpine — but alpine is blocked by the NFR5 guard so this is never reached).

interfaces.name: Provisioner._generate_playbook (deferred line)
interfaces.behavior_change: DEFERRED to Story 05 per team-lead instructions: do NOT add an 'opencode_version' Ansible var in this story. The role accepts the literal sentinel 'latest' from its own default in this story (see role contract below).

interfaces.name: roles/opencode-ubuntu/tasks/main.yml (new file)
interfaces.behavior_change: 1. Set fact opencode_target_version, defaulting to playbook var 'opencode_version' if defined, else 'latest'. 2. When 'latest', resolve to tag_name from https://api.github.com/repos/anomalyco/opencode/releases/latest via Ansible uri module (or curl via command); strip leading 'v'. 3. Run the official install script (curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash) as the unprivileged VM user with environment OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin and OPENCODE_VERSION (or pass --version) and the --no-modify-path flag. 4. Verify with 'opencode --version'. 5. changed_when set so reruns with same version are no-op (idempotent). NO opencode auth login. NO config file pre-population. NO Node.js dependency.

interfaces.name: wizard.run() framework multi-select
interfaces.behavior_change: Add ('opencode', 'opencode', False) tuple alongside the existing ('playwright', 'playwright', False). Frameworks-only edit; no harness step in this story (Story 03).

interfaces.name: detect/wizard_integration.py framework-options lists
interfaces.signature: wizard_integration.py at lines ~172, ~382, ~738 (the three _menu_multi_select 'Select frameworks:' calls)
interfaces.behavior_change: Each of the three multi-select option lists gains the 'opencode' entry. Pre-checked state mirrors playwright pattern: False for new flows; 'opencode' in detected_tools / 'opencode' in config.frameworks / 'opencode' in merged_frameworks for the three respective contexts.

data_flow: User picks 'opencode' in wizard frameworks multi-select OR sets frameworks: [opencode, ...] in .clauded.yaml -> Config.frameworks contains 'opencode' -> Provisioner.run() checks NFR5 guard (vm_distro != 'alpine') -> _get_base_roles() appends 'opencode' -> _apply_distro_suffix maps to 'opencode-ubuntu' -> ansible-playbook runs roles/opencode-ubuntu/tasks/main.yml -> install script downloads opencode binary into $HOME/.local/bin -> 'opencode --version' verifies on PATH. Launch entrypoint unchanged in this story (still claude).
files_to_modify: src/clauded/provisioner.py (add 'opencode' to _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS, append 'opencode' to roles in _get_base_roles when in frameworks, add NFR5 alpine guard in run() — before role list construction)
src/clauded/wizard.py (add 'opencode' tuple to the framework multi-select around line 195)
src/clauded/detect/wizard_integration.py (add 'opencode' tuple to all three _menu_multi_select 'Select frameworks:' option lists at ~lines 172, 382, 738)
tests/test_provisioner.py (paired-include-exclude pattern, alpine guard test, parametrize 16 subsets for AC-021)
tests/test_wizard.py (recording-select-side-effect: assert 'opencode' present in offered framework options)
tests/test_detect_integration.py (assert 'opencode' present in framework options for all three integration flows)
CHANGELOG.md ([Unreleased] Added entry: opencode framework option for Ubuntu VMs)
implementation_strategy:
TDD per-component:

1. Tests-first for provisioner (paired include/exclude, no-node assertion, alpine guard, 16-subset orthogonality parametrization, _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS mapping). RED.
2. Update src/clauded/provisioner.py: add 'opencode' to _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS, append 'opencode' branch to _get_base_roles (no node auto-add), add NFR5 alpine guard in run() that raises ConfigValidationError with --distro ubuntu in the message. GREEN.
3. Tests-first for wizard frameworks-list (recording-select-side-effect: assert offered options include opencode). RED.
4. Update src/clauded/wizard.py: add 'opencode' option to framework multi-select. GREEN.
5. Tests-first for wizard_integration framework lists (3 separate test cases). RED.
6. Update src/clauded/detect/wizard_integration.py: add 'opencode' option in all three flows. GREEN.
7. Create src/clauded/roles/opencode-ubuntu/tasks/main.yml mirroring codex-ubuntu structure but using the opencode install script + GitHub API tag resolution + $HOME/.local/bin. The role accepts the literal 'latest' sentinel via opencode_version playbook var (default 'latest') — Story 05 will wire the var; Story 01's role default keeps the playbook independent.
8. Add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] Added entry.
9. Run `make check`; verify all 935 baseline tests still pass plus new ones; coverage stays >= 80%.

In-flight QA per integration-architect protocol: ollama review then codex review on the implementation diff before handoff to verifier.
